pattern: Encode the parity of each vowel's occurrence count into a 5-bit bitmask; when the same mask recurs, the substring between its two appearances has every vowel count balanced (even), so track the earliest index of each mask.
hints: Two positions with the same vowel-parity bitmask mean every vowel appeared an even number of times in between — that is your candidate substring.
Maintain a running XOR mask: for each character, flip the bit of its vowel (if it is a vowel). The mask after position i captures the parity state up to i.
Store the first occurrence index of each mask value (initialize mask 0 at index -1 for the empty prefix); whenever the mask repeats, update the answer with the gap.

edge_inputs.description: example with a long valid substring
answer is a short vowel-balanced prefix
no vowels — entire string is valid
single vowel repeated odd times — no full-length answer
single vowel repeated even times — full string valid
length-1 string that is a vowel
all five vowels each odd times — answer is empty or requires careful check
all five vowels each appearing twice — whole string valid
